task:
Build a CLI tool called ttrack for time tracking in Python.
Use typer for CLI, sqlite3 (stdlib) for storage, rich for output, pytest + ruff + mypy for quality.
Use pyproject.toml with all tool configs.

Stack: Python 3.11+, Typer, SQLite3 (stdlib), Rich, pytest, ruff, mypy.

IMPORTANT: FEATURES.md is already created in the workspace by the setup script.
Do NOT recreate, overwrite, reorder, modify, or expand it.
Do NOT add features beyond F-001..F-008. Do NOT split features into sub-features.
Do NOT add separate 'test coverage' or 'tooling' features.
The 8 features are fixed. Implement them as described, no more.

Feature overview (read FEATURES.md for authoritative spec):

F-001: Scaffold + SQLite init + `ttrack init`
  Create src/ttrack/ package (__init__.py, db.py, main.py).
  SQLite DB (ttrack.db default, overridable via TTRACK_DB env var).
  Tables:
    entries(id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, project TEXT NOT NULL, task TEXT DEFAULT '',
      tags TEXT DEFAULT '', note TEXT DEFAULT '', start_time TEXT ISO8601 NOT NULL,
      end_time TEXT ISO8601 NULLABLE, created_at TEXT ISO8601).
  `ttrack init` creates DB and table, prints confirmation, idempotent.
  Tests: init creates file, second init is idempotent, tables exist.

F-002: Start timer: `ttrack start PROJECT [--task TASK] [--tags CSV]`
  Insert new entry with start_time=now (UTC ISO8601), end_time=NULL.
  If another timer is running (end_time IS NULL), refuse and print warning.
  --task: optional task description. --tags: comma-separated tags.
  Prints: "Started [PROJECT] at HH:MM:SS" on success.
  Tests: start creates entry, cannot start while running, start with all flags.

F-003: Stop timer: `ttrack stop [--note TEXT]`
  Find the running entry (end_time IS NULL), set end_time=now.
  --note: optional note to attach.
  Print: "Stopped [PROJECT] — DURATION" where DURATION is HH:MM:SS.
  Error if no timer is running.
  Tests: stop sets end_time, stop with note, stop when no timer errors.

F-004: Status: `ttrack status`
  If a timer is running: show project, task, tags, start time, elapsed duration.
  If no timer running: print "No timer running."
  Output: rich Panel with fields.
  Tests: status when running, status when idle.

F-005: Log: `ttrack log [--project PROJECT] [--from YYYY-MM-DD] [--to YYYY-MM-DD] [--tag TAG] [--last N]`
  List completed entries matching filters. Default: last 10 entries.
  --tag: substring match in tags field. Dates: filter on start_time date part.
  Output: rich Table (ID | Project | Task | Duration | Tags | Date).
  Duration = end_time - start_time formatted as HH:MM:SS. Running entries show "running".
  Tests: log all, project filter, date range, tag filter, --last override.

F-006: Summary: `ttrack summary [--day YYYY-MM-DD | --week YYYY-MM-DD | --month YYYY-MM]`
  Aggregate total time by project for the period.
  --day: single day (default: today). --week: Mon-Sun containing date. --month: calendar month.
  Output: rich Table (Project | Total Time | Entries | % of Total).
  Footer row with grand total. Only completed entries counted.
  Tests: day summary, week summary, month summary, multiple projects.

F-007: Report: `ttrack report [--format json|csv] [--from YYYY-MM-DD] [--to YYYY-MM-DD] [--project PROJECT] [--output FILE]`
  Export filtered completed entries.
  json: JSON array with all fields + computed duration_seconds.
  csv: CSV with header row.
  Default format: json. Default output: stdout. --output writes to file.
  Tests: JSON schema correct, CSV has headers, file output, filtered report.

F-008: Edit entry: `ttrack edit ID [--project PROJECT] [--task TASK] [--tags CSV] [--note TEXT]`
  Update fields of an existing entry by ID.
  Only update fields that are explicitly passed. Leave others unchanged.
  Error if ID not found.
  Print: "Updated entry #ID" on success.
  Tests: edit project, edit tags, edit note, edit nonexistent ID errors, partial update.

setup: python -m pip install typer rich pytest ruff mypy pip-audit

mkdir -p tests && printf '# Feature Tracker

## Product: Time Tracker CLI

### Status Legend
- `not-started` — Not yet begun
- `in-progress` — Currently being implemented
- `done` — Implemented and tested
- `blocked` — Blocked by external dependency

### Features

| ID | Feature | Status | Priority | Notes |
|----|---------|--------|----------|-------|
| F-001 | Scaffold + SQLite init + `ttrack init` | not-started | P0 | src/ttrack/ package, entries table, init cmd |
| F-002 | Start timer: `ttrack start PROJECT [--task] [--tags]` | not-started | P0 | insert entry with start_time, refuse if running |
| F-003 | Stop timer: `ttrack stop [--note]` | not-started | P0 | set end_time, calc duration, error if none running |
| F-004 | Status: `ttrack status` | not-started | P1 | show running timer or "No timer running" |
| F-005 | Log: `ttrack log [--project] [--from] [--to] [--tag] [--last N]` | not-started | P1 | filtered entry list, rich Table, duration calc |
| F-006 | Summary: `ttrack summary [--day | --week | --month]` | not-started | P1 | time by project, total, percentage |
| F-007 | Report: `ttrack report [--format json|csv] [--from] [--to] [--output]` | not-started | P2 | export with duration_seconds, stdout or file |
| F-008 | Edit: `ttrack edit ID [--project] [--task] [--tags] [--note]` | not-started | P2 | partial update by ID, error if not found |

### Backlog
_(none — feature set is fixed at 8)_
' > FEATURES.md

program_md:
# PROGRAM.md — Time Tracker CLI (ttrack-001)

## Product: Time Tracker CLI

## Objective

Maximize composite quality score through hypothesis-driven mutations.
Each loop must produce a small, testable increment — not a large batch.

## Mutation Scope

- Max files changed per loop: 3
- Max files created per loop: 2
- Max diff lines per loop: 200
- Max endpoint/route changes per loop: 1

## Eval Protocol

weights:
  tests: 0.35
  lint: 0.20
  typecheck: 0.15
  coverage: 0.05
  security: 0.05
  feature_coverage: 0.20

## Keep/Discard Policy

- keep_threshold: score_after > score_before
- tie_policy: discard

## Budget

- max_loops: 12
- max_wall_seconds: 14400
- max_discards_in_a_row: 3

## Stop Conditions

- target_score: 1.00
- min_improvement_delta: 0.01
- max_plateau_loops: 3
- consecutive_discards >= max_discards_in_a_row

## Hypothesis Sources

- FEATURES.md, eval failures, coverage gaps, EVALS/ledger.jsonl

## Arena Contract

- **FIRST: Read EVALS/ledger.jsonl before choosing your hypothesis.**
  Find the most recent entry. Check the "verdict" field.
  If verdict was "discard_audit", your next hypothesis MUST reduce scope (fewer files).
  Write your hypothesis as: "Last verdict: <verdict> | Response: <your adaptation>"
- Choose exactly one baseline feature (F-001..F-008) or one blocking defect per loop.
- Before making changes, search the codebase. Do not assume missing implementation.
- Do not create scratch, debug, or temp files.
- Do not edit scoring files, eval scripts, or EVALS/features-baseline.json.
- Do not add, remove, reorder, or split features in FEATURES.md.
- Run the smallest relevant test first, then full eval.
- Update FEATURES.md status only for F-001..F-008.
- KEEP only if score improves and audit passes.
- If tests break, fix them in the SAME loop before moving on.
- Prefer depth (thorough tests for one feature) over breadth.
